canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, Min-K% is consistently defined as a type of membership inference attack. The method operates by averaging the losses of the k% least likely tokens, which are those with the highest loss values. This technique is explicitly based on the assumption "that non-members contain more high-loss outliers" ("Confounding Factors in Relating Model Performance to Morphology"). As an instrument, Min-K% is used to measure the construct of `Privacy leakage`. The provided data also indicates a relationship between Min-K% and the "Min-K% method".
papers: A Graph-Theoretical Framework for Analyzing the Behavior of Causal Language Models
Confounding Factors in Relating Model Performance to Morphology
TAPS: Tool-Augmented Personalisation via Structured Tagging

definitions.text: A membership inference attack that averages the losses of the k% least likely tokens (highest loss) under the assumption that non-members contain more high-loss outliers.
definitions.paper: Confounding Factors in Relating Model Performance to Morphology

relationships.paper: Min-K%++: Improved Baseline for Pre-Training Data Detection from Large Language Models

iclr25: Min-K%++: Improved Baseline for Pre-Training Data Detection from Large Language Models

relationships.paper: Underestimated Privacy Risks for Minority Populations in Large Language Model Unlearning

icml25: Underestimated Privacy Risks for Minority Populations in Large Language Model Unlearning
